description: T99 SeatReservationHandler MUST atomically reserve a seat (TTL hold via F274) BEFORE initiating payment capture. Natural order pay-then-seat creates a double-booking race: two users pay simultaneously for the last seat, both payments succeed, both seat claims succeed, capacity becomes -1. Two people hold tickets to the same last seat. No error anywhere. SILENT_FAILURE: passes unit tests, fails under concurrent load on last ticket.
blockConditions: payment capture initiated before seat reservation TTL hold
no TTL hold on seat before payment processing
passConditions: reserveSeat(TTL) via F274 called before enqueuePayment()
seat hold confirmed before payment initiated
resolution: T99 calls reserveSeat(TTL) before enqueuePayment(). Line index of reserveSeat < line index of enqueuePayment. Payment initiated AFTER seat hold confirmed. Absent TTL hold = BUILD_FAILURE.

description: T113 FraudDetectorHandler runs inline within T99. When fraud service (F283) is unavailable, T113 MUST fail open — purchase proceeds + audit event emitted. Three conditions required simultaneously: (1) try/catch around F283 call, (2) on catch: return { passed: true } allowing purchase, (3) on catch: emit FraudCheckFailed(service_unavailable). Missing any one condition = SILENT_FAILURE.
blockConditions: F283 unavailability blocks all purchases (fail closed)
catch block returns { passed: false } preventing purchase
FraudCheckFailed audit event absent on service unavailability
passConditions: on catch: return { passed: true } allowing purchase to proceed
on catch: emit FraudCheckFailed({ reason: 'service_unavailable', purchaseId })
resolution: T113 catch block: emit FraudCheckFailed({ reason: 'service_unavailable', purchaseId }) AND return { passed: true }. catch returning false = BUILD_FAILURE.

description: T105 ComplianceEscalationHandler after max retries MUST simultaneously emit RefundFailed AND push to F284 (manual review queue). Both actions required — neither alone is sufficient. PER_ATTEMPT_IDEMPOTENCY: idempotency key = hash(tenantId + purchaseId + 'refund-attempt-' + N). Global key without attempt counter deduplicates all retries as duplicates of attempt 1.
blockConditions: RefundFailed emitted without F284 manual review queue push
global idempotency key without attempt counter deduplicates all retries as attempt 1
passConditions: on max retry exhaustion: emit RefundFailed AND push to F284
per-attempt idempotency key includes attempt counter N: hash(tenantId + purchaseId + 'refund-attempt-' + N)
resolution: On max retry exhaustion: emit RefundFailed AND push to F284. Per-attempt idempotency key includes attempt counter N. Missing F284 push = BUILD_FAILURE.

description: T108 GroupBookingCoordinatorHandler MUST wrap ALL group ticket issuances in a single database transaction. Any member failure triggers full rollback — no partial success. SILENT_FAILURE: for...of loop outside db.transaction() compiles and passes all unit tests. Partial success only visible when one member fails mid-loop in production.
blockConditions: for...of ticket issuance loop placed outside db.transaction()
partial group booking success permitted without rollback
passConditions: all group ticket issuances wrapped in single db.transaction()
any single member failure triggers full rollback for all members
resolution: T108 uses db.transaction(() => [issue each member ticket]). All issuances inside one transaction. Loop outside transaction = BUILD_FAILURE.

description: T111 PayoutOrchestrator payout amount MUST be read from stored RevenueRecord — NOT recalculated independently. T111 must NOT call T112 again. The stored RevenueRecord (written by T110) contains feeBreakdown.totalFee. payoutAmount = revenueRecord.netAmount. If T111 recalculates fees independently, fee divergence occurs when FREEDOM config rates change: two sources of truth produce accounting discrepancy.
blockConditions: T111 recalculates fees independently from T112
T111 calls T112 again for fee calculation instead of reading stored RevenueRecord
passConditions: T111 reads payoutAmount from stored RevenueRecord.netAmount
minimum payout threshold from FREEDOM config
resolution: T111 reads payoutAmount from stored RevenueRecord. F281 INJECTABLE. Minimum threshold from FREEDOM config. SETNX on (purchaseId + payout). Inline fee recalculation = BUILD_FAILURE.

description: F275 QR token generation (T102 QRCodeGeneratorHandler) and validation (T103 QRCodeValidatorHandler) is PLATFORM-ONLY. No tenant factory swap permitted. QR token integrity is a security primitive: a tenant swapping F275 could generate tokens that bypass validation, enabling ticket fraud.
blockConditions: F275 declared INJECTABLE in T102 contract
F275 declared INJECTABLE in T103 contract
tenant swap of QR token factory permitted
passConditions: F275 declared PLATFORM-ONLY in both T102 and T103 contracts
no INJECTABLE declaration of F275 anywhere in FLOW-09
resolution: F275 declared PLATFORM-ONLY in both T102 and T103 contracts. Any INJECTABLE declaration of F275 = BUILD_FAILURE.

description: Ticket purchase, payment, refund, and revenue records are PRIVATE (per-user per-event). T110 reads GLOBAL event aggregate (intentional â€” published revenue data). T112 is inline-pure â€” writes no records, scope check trivially passes. T114 ComplianceReporter pushes to PLATFORM-ONLY F284 â€” platform scope, not tenant. PRIVATE records must never be stored as GLOBAL.
blockConditions: ticket purchase record stored with knowledgeScope:GLOBAL
payment record stored with knowledgeScope:GLOBAL
refund record stored with knowledgeScope:GLOBAL
payout record stored with knowledgeScope:GLOBAL
tenantId absent from any stored record
tenantId sourced from request parameter instead of ALS
T112 (inline-pure) calls storeDocument
passConditions: All ticket, payment, refund, and revenue records stored with knowledgeScope: 'PRIVATE'
tenantId sourced exclusively from cls.get().tenantId (AsyncLocalStorage) in all T99-T118 services
T110 reads GLOBAL event aggregates only — all write records remain PRIVATE with tenantId from ALS
